tweets.text:
Retail investors are cashing-in profits:

Retail investors sold -$1.1 billion in equities last week, posting the largest weekly sale of the year.

This comes after 3 consecutive weekly purchases, totaling +$1.6 billion.

Single stocks led the outflows at -$1.7 billion, while equity ETFs attracted +$571 million in retail inflows.

At the same time, institutional investors bought +$219 million, following 3 consecutive weeks of over +$3.0 billion in purchases.

Hedge funds also turned buyers, acquiring +$686 million, following -$4.6 billion in sales in the preceding week.

In total, investors sold -$1.9 billion in single stocks and purchased +$1.6 billion in equity ETFs last week.

We are seeing historic flows in both directions.
tweets.full_text:
Retail investors are cashing-in profits:

Retail investors sold -$1.1 billion in equities last week, posting the largest weekly sale of the year.

This comes after 3 consecutive weekly purchases, totaling +$1.6 billion.

Single stocks led the outflows at -$1.7 billion, while equity ETFs attracted +$571 million in retail inflows.

At the same time, institutional investors bought +$219 million, following 3 consecutive weeks of over +$3.0 billion in purchases.

Hedge funds also turned buyers, acquiring +$686 million, following -$4.6 billion in sales in the preceding week.

In total, investors sold -$1.9 billion in single stocks and purchased +$1.6 billion in equity ETFs last week.

We are seeing historic flows in both directions.

tweets.text:
We partnered with Pylon to investigate whether AI is replacing or augmenting jobs in customer support.

The resounding answer: AI is acting much more as an invisible triage agent or copilot than it is as an end-to-end support handler.

If your company as a whole is pretty AI-native, there’s a much higher likelihood that your customer support AI will be good at resolving questions end-to-end.

In particular, “AI-resolved” tickets are more likely to be actual resolutions, as opposed to “the customer gave up and dropped off.”

But if you’re a really important, big-spending B2B customer? You’re probably still gonna get a human.

Full breakdown: https://t.co/eTRff9stMu
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
From the beginning at Pylon, we've had this conviction that you can't just run B2B support on platforms made for B2C. 

Why?

Account context. 

Context is everything when your customers...
→ Are accounts with multiple stakeholders
→ Sign multi-year contracts
→ Pay you 6+ https://t.co/fqFHNQ7ViX
tweets.full_text:
We partnered with Pylon to investigate whether AI is replacing or augmenting jobs in customer support.

The resounding answer: AI is acting much more as an invisible triage agent or copilot than it is as an end-to-end support handler.

If your company as a whole is pretty AI-native, there’s a much higher likelihood that your customer support AI will be good at resolving questions end-to-end.

In particular, “AI-resolved” tickets are more likely to be actual resolutions, as opposed to “the customer gave up and dropped off.”

But if you’re a really important, big-spending B2B customer? You’re probably still gonna get a human.

Full breakdown: https://t.co/eTRff9stMu

tweets.text:
Crypto fund outflows are accelerating:

Crypto ETFs posted -$1.5 billion in outflows last week, the largest weekly outflow since February.

This follows -$1.1 billion withdrawn in the preceding week, bringing the 2-week total to -$2.6 billion, the 3rd largest in at least a year.

Bitcoin again led the outflows at -$1.3 billion, the largest weekly sale of 2026.

This was followed by Ethereum at -$223 million, bringing the 2-week total to -$472 million.

As a result, Bitcoin year-to-date inflows are down to +$2.6 billion.

Crypto market momentum has stalled again.
tweets.full_text:

Bitcoin again led the outflows at -$1.3 billion, the largest weekly sale of 2026.

This was followed by Ethereum at -$223 million, bringing the 2-week total to -$472 million.

As a result, Bitcoin year-to-date inflows are down to +$2.6 billion.

tweets.text:
BREAKING: US Treasury Secretary Bessent issues a warning to Oman:

"The US will not tolerate any effort to impose a tolling system in the Strait of Hormuz. Oman, in particular, should know that the US Treasury will aggressively target any actors involved - directly or indirectly - in facilitating tolls for the Strait and any willing partners will be penalized."

Yesterday, President Trump threatened to "blow up" Oman if they do not "behave just like everybody else."

tweets.text: '90s Active Stars Reborn as ETF Juggernauts After Slashing Fees.. New from me on on how CapGp, Franklin, T Rowe, etc are all QUICKLY climbing up the ETF aum leaderboard, getting thier mojo back, thanks to more moderate fees (their avg ETF fee is half that of their avg MF fee). That said, many seeing offsetting outflows (or worse) from their mutual funds. Here's 5yr flows for each firm and each product type. Fidelity outlier thanks to massive cheap index fund biz.

tweets.text:
As price contracted to $72.9k, the supply held at a loss rose to 8.33M BTC, up from 7.75M at $76.6k.

The delta implies roughly 580k BTC was accumulated in the $72.9k–$76.6k range, a local top-heavy structure now fully underwater.
This cohort adds to near-term sell pressure as holders reassess their positions into the correction.

📉https://t.co/iKBDS0dbt2
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
At current price, ~7.75M BTC are held at a loss.

This supply overhang is a structural feature of bear markets, typically resolved only as weaker hands capitulate.

📉https://t.co/1ZdVx8qNpP https://t.co/msqr8e7mPw

thread_tweets.text:
The VanEck Hyperliquid ETN (VHRL) is live and available for trading on Deutsche Börse Xetra (VHRL GY in EUR) as of today. 
Hyperliquid operates the largest decentralized perpetual futures exchange, and built on its own purpose-built Layer-1 blockchain with sub-second finality and CEX-rivaling execution speed. 
Historically, over 97% of protocol fees are funding direct HYPE token buybacks through the Assistance Fund, therefore the token's demand is structurally linked to platform usage. 
HYPE is the native token of the Hyperliquid ecosystem (HyperCore and HyperEVM). 
The VanEck Hyperliquid ETN is a fully-collateralized exchange traded note that invests in the HYPE Token.

tweets.text:
May 27, 2026: VIX at 16.29 — investors still aren’t paying up for protection.

Low volatility can support higher prices for a while. But low volatility alongside weakening internals is where reversals can become sharp. If breadth keeps fading with VIX this suppressed, that’s a warning, not confirmation. A move above 20–25 would be the first sign fear is returning.

Track it daily and import the data into your workflow through SentimenTrader’s Light Data API: https://t.co/nq1PURWpKJ

tweets.text: Good Morning from Germany, where the welfare state keeps expanding: Govt cash social benefits jumped 5.9% in 2025 to €751.2bn; far above the long-term avg of 3.4%. tweets.text:
The gap between S&P 500 forward P/E and the VIX just widened back to nearly 6 points.

For $SPY, the index was higher 81% of the time a year later.

But the VIX surged a median 47% over the next 3 months.

Higher. Bumpier.

Read full analysis: https://t.co/iJUI9CJyKS https://t.co/Ln2doLAIOB
